description: Dr Aislinn Rice has over 25 years’ experience in leadership positions across small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) and global organisations in the technology sector. She was appointed to the Board of QUBIS from 2012 to 2018, the commercialisation arm of Queen’s University Belfast ( QUB ). Aislinn is currently Director of Software NI and Non-Executive Director at Analytics Engines. She holds a PhD in Chemistry from QUB .  More recently, Aislinn was awarded with the Digital DNA Special Recognition Award for continued commitment and contribution to the NI Technology ecosystem. She was named as 2025’s Outstanding Woman in Tech at the Northern Ireland Women in Tech Awards.

details.body:
Dr Aislinn Rice has over 25 years’ experience in leadership positions across small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) and global organisations in the technology sector. She was appointed to the Board of QUBIS from 2012 to 2018, the commercialisation arm of Queen’s University Belfast (QUB). Aislinn is currently Director of Software NI and Non-Executive Director at Analytics Engines. She holds a PhD in Chemistry from QUB. 

More recently, Aislinn was awarded with the Digital DNA Special Recognition Award for continued commitment and contribution to the NI Technology ecosystem. She was named as 2025’s Outstanding Woman in Tech at the Northern Ireland Women in Tech Awards.

Dr Aislinn Rice has over 25 years’ experience in leadership positions across small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) and global organisations in the technology sector. She was appointed to the Board of QUBIS from 2012 to 2018, the commercialisation arm of Queen’s University Belfast (QUB). Aislinn is currently Director of Software NI and Non-Executive Director at Analytics Engines. She holds a PhD in Chemistry from QUB. 

More recently, Aislinn was awarded with the Digital DNA Special Recognition Award for continued commitment and contribution to the NI Technology ecosystem. She was named as 2025’s Outstanding Woman in Tech at the Northern Ireland Women in Tech Awards.

Members of the Industrial Strategy Advisory Council (ISAC) contribute to the development and long-term delivery of HM Government’s industrial strategy. They work with the ISAC chair and other ISAC members to produce independent, evidence-based recommendations that directly inform government policy.

role_appointments.title: Dr. Aislinn Rice - Industrial Strategy Advisory Council member

title: Dr Aislinn Rice
